verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is predominantly an ER single-pass TM protein (type I), but a documented PM-resident pool (STIM1PM) exists in certain cell types where it is stably inserted into the plasma membrane via its own TM domain with an extracellular N-terminus, stabilized by N-glycosylation. Additionally, upon ER Ca2+ store depletion, the ER pool translocates to ER-PM junctions. The PM-inserted fraction is not the dominant localization across all cell types, making this contextual (dual_localization) rather than a constitutive surface protein.
